title: Tactus host-module mechanism (Tier 1)
description:
# Tactus host-module mechanism (Tier 1)

## Purpose

Upstream Tactus work to allow host applications (Plexus, score-processor-lambda, future hosts) to register Python-backed modules accessible via Lua `require()` from sandboxed Tactus code, without adding host-specific code to the Tactus standard library.

## Background

Tactus already has `require()` working with a Python-backed `package.preload` searcher (see `tactus/core/lua_sandbox.py` `_setup_safe_require`, `_setup_python_stdlib_loader` and `tactus/stdlib/loader.py` `StdlibModuleLoader`). The searcher is currently restricted to names starting with `tactus.` loaded from `tactus/stdlib/*.py`.

What's missing is a public host API to register additional Python-backed modules from outside Tactus, with name namespaces other than `tactus.*`.

## Scope (Tier 1, minimal)

- Add `TactusRuntime.register_python_module(name, module, version=None)` host API.
- Extend the Python-backed `package.preload` searcher to consult a host-registered map in addition to `tactus/stdlib/`.
- Relax the `tactus.` prefix restriction to allow arbitrary registered names.
- Tests modeled on `tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py`.
- Optional hardening: remove `string.dump` from sandbox while we are in there.

## Out of scope (Tier 2, separate future initiative)

Real package system: manifests, semver versioning, dependency graphs, file-system discovery (`tactus_modules/`), capability declarations, central registry, `tactus install` CLI, polyglot package format. Tier 1 is forward-compatible with all of these — a Tier 2 `TactusPackage` registered programmatically by the host would be equivalent to a Tier 1 `register_python_module` call.

## Estimate

~50 lines of Tactus code + ~100 lines of tests. 1–2 days of focused work.

## Coordination

Tactus is internal — this lands as a normal Tactus stdlib enhancement. Plexus pins to a Tactus feature branch during development; Tactus releases the feature in a normal version when stable.
